title: GEO-Bench Earth-Monitoring Foundation-Model Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Use for task definitions and benchmark motivation.
Use for dataset/task description and citation.

instructions: Use the official repository loader and inspect access requirements for the selected task.
Start with one classification or segmentation task whose data can be fetched without special credentials.
Record task name, sensor/modality, bands, geographic region, timestamp/season, label taxonomy, and split.

scale.download_size: multi-task earth-observation benchmark; component datasets vary in size and access

recommended_axes.name: geographic_support
recommended_axes.definition: Distance or region overlap between train and test image chips.

recommended_axes.name: sensor_modality_support
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity across multispectral, SAR, hyperspectral, elevation, or cloud-probability modalities.

recommended_axes.name: land_cover_or_task_taxonomy_support
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity in class hierarchy, label prevalence, and task semantics.

recommended_axes.name: seasonal_and_temporal_support
recommended_axes.definition: Temporal, seasonal, or acquisition-date distance from training observations.

useful_for: Auditing earth-observation foundation models across task, geography, sensor, and season.
Testing whether geospatial benchmark splits are points on continuous spatial/sensor novelty curves.
Providing a broad non-biology SPECTRA capsule.
leakage_risks: Nearby or overlapping tiles can leak spatial context.
Component datasets may have different label schemas and split semantics.
Pretrained earth-observation models may have seen the same public imagery.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip found GEO-Bench in earth-observation foundation-model searches; official repository confirms a reusable benchmark loader and task suite.
